column1: Test removal of different sections of LP
column26:
Because of the length of the LP, there's a high chance that some of the sections added are actually hurting conversion. So we're testing out removing pieces of the LP one at a time in the variants to see which sections should be removed for maximizing conversion.

Removing different 6 sections within the LP:
1. 5 Reasons
2. What to look forward  to
3. How to use earlybird
4. Here's Where The Magic Happens
5. Morning cocktail
6. Earlybird vs Others

column1: Social Proofing + CTA add to HP Banner
column26: Currently HP Banner has a nice video but is lacking any form of CTA or social proofing. Adding a CTA while highlighting benefits (Made-in-America, 60 Day Guarantee, Free Shipping) and a carousel for reviews.

column1: Social Proof Counter with Velocity Indicators
column26:
"Real-time" activity signals tap into herding behavior psychology. The test replaces the static user review above the title and tests it with:
A) Live counter showing "X people started their EarlyBird ritual this week"
B) Conversion-focused statistic ""2 out of 3 people who try EarlyBird become subscribers! :star:"
C) Savings Callout: "EarlyBirds save an average of $87/month on coffee shop visits! :moneybag:
D) "Join 50,000+ morning optimizers who start their day with intention! :sun_with_face: The sunrise club that's changing how the world wakes up."

Price framing around daily use creates reference points that make premium products feel more accessible. Per-morning framing typically reduces price objections.

Adding text underneath the title to compare with Starbucks, then adjusting pricing to be more streamlined, and having the serving price within the add-to-cart button.

column1: Follow Up - Section Removal vs Control
column26: We had tested removing each individual sections and 2 sections were highlighted as major improvements after they were removed. The sections are: "Morning Cocktail" and "EarlyBird vs Others". We'll be conducting a 50/50 comparing the original vs the variant with these sections removed.

column26: The previous test was unsuccessful but our thoughts are the CTA was forcing users to skip through the learning process whereas the social proofing should've improved the conversion by building up trust. We're testing this again with the social proofing alone.

column26: We're implementing strikethrough original pricing in the shopping cart to better showcase the discount value. Currently, the cart only displays sale prices ($68 for starter kit, $19 for sample pack) without showing the original prices ($98, $29). This test adds crossed-out original pricing next to current prices in both line items and subtotals to emphasize the savings customers are receiving.

We're adding a free shipping notification bar at the top of the cart experience. For sample pack customers (who don't qualify for free shipping), the bar will inform them they can upgrade to the starter kit to receive free shipping, with a button linking back to product selection. For starter kit customers (who already qualify), it will confirm their free shipping benefit to reinforce their purchase decision.
Previous tests suggests users respond positively to social proofing and there is currently no upsells so we'll try both within their cart.

column1: Change hero CTA button text copy
column25: Changing the hero CTA from “Shop Now” to “Get 20% Off Your First Order” will increase add-to-cart rate by 18% by creating stronger urgency and perceived value for new visitors.
column26: New visitors often hesitate with generic “Shop Now” CTAs due to lack of immediate incentive. A discount-focused message like “Get 20% Off Your First Order” creates perceived value, lowers perceived risk, and builds urgency for first-time buyers, likely lifting add-to-cart rate among cold traffic.

column25: Replacing the sticky “Add to Cart” button with a floating one that includes a mini product image + “Only 3 left!” stock warning will boost mobile conversions by 22% by reducing friction and adding scarcity FOMO.
column26: Mobile users frequently abandon carts when the sticky Add to Cart button feels disconnected from the product. Adding a floating button with a mini product image keeps visual context, while the “Only 3 left!” scarcity trigger creates FOMO and urgency. This combo reduces decision friction and boosts impulse conversions on mobile.

column1: Main product copy re-write, 3 Top Selling Products
column26: Only 10% of users actually see the main product description, while the imagery and content can be instrumental to motivate purchases.
Proactively removing objections in the Cart are instrumental to motivate shoppers to complete their purchase.

column1:
Mega menu redesign + restructure
(Desktop and Mobile)
column26: The current navigation is both wide and deep. We believe a simpler hierarchy, fewer levels, and key visual elements will aid navigation.
Extending returns to 45 days enhances customer confidence and satisfaction, potentially reducing purchase hesitations.
Eliminating outbound links in product descriptions keeps users focused on-site, potentially increasing conversion rates.
A dynamic shipping bar that tracks spend motivates additional purchases to reach free shipping thresholds, potentially increasing AOV.
Displaying a 3-day delivery estimate for USA orders sets clear expectations, enhancing customer satisfaction and trust.
Removing the newsletter popup on PDP pages reduces distractions, focusing user attention on product details and purchase.

column26: The 2x2 grid has a significantly higher interaction rate even though it's lower down on the page, we think think this is a signal that moving it higher will improve page performance
We believe users will have a significantly better experience when removing the subheading underneath every product on the collections page and improve conversion rates.
We think cleaning up the PDP structure, wasted white space & using it to display key customer reviews will improve conversion rates
We think a little nudge in the cart reminding users of the free shipping threshold offer may increase average order value
Users who use search features convert higher than any others, a prominent search bar makes it easier & faster to get from the first page > final purchase
We believe adding "Quick collection" filters at the top of collections pages will improve navigation & conversion rate
When will my product be delivered? Is a question top of mind for every prospective customer, this information is not currently displayed and that may be negatively impacting performance
The current upsells in cart for the Ziggy product are not aligned with what products are commonly ordered with the Ziggy
We believe moving the "You are perfect" section under the hero section will increase the perceived value of the product prior to users navigating to them
Testing a sticky CTA to determine its effect on conversion rates.
Incorporating a USP checklist in the hero to enhance clarity and conversion.

column1: Removing different sections within the Tiktok LP
column26: The LP is quite long. There is a high chance that sections of the page aren't all contributing to the CVR. The goal is to test the removal of 5 different sections (5 variants).

We're going to highlight positive KPIs found within their user research study. We'll present this information in two ways to avoid overwhelming them:

First, we'll showcase four key benefits within the image carousel. Then, we'll reinforce these same four benefits underneath the order form.

This approach lets them grasp the main benefits without having to thoroughly review the entire study.
